name: Tempura Ichika
tagline: Oahu's only dedicated tempura omakase — a Tokyo-caliber counter experience hidden behind a nondescript Piikoi St facade.
mustOrder: tempura omakase ($160/person) — the only option, and worth every dollar
seasonal vegetables — the chef's batter work elevates each ingredient
sushi omakase at Sushi Ichika (same address, separate experience)
parking: Street parking on Piikoi St; nearby paid lots
talkStory: Chef Kiyoshi Chikano, who trained under Michelin-starred masters in Japan, opened this intimate counter in a location most people walk right past. Inside, it's 8 seats facing the chef as he fries each piece of tempura to order — shrimp, seasonal fish, vegetables — with the precision and reverence you'd find in Tokyo's Ebisu neighborhood. At $160/person it's a splurge, but locals who know Japanese food treat this as the real deal without Waikiki markup. They also run Sushi Ichika at the same address for a separate sushi omakase experience. Reserve ahead.
